_doc: Data sources planned for comp-014 multi-database aggregation. Phase 1 (this commit) registers sources and access methods; Phase 2 executes the aggregation. Per Open Enzyme/CLAUDE.md global-multilingual-research rule, non-English-corpus sources are first-class — not 'language barrier' caveats.
_phase_1_status: scope only — no DB calls executed in Phase 1; Phase 2a starts now

FDA_GRAS_Notice_Inventory.use: Inclusion list — fungal species with explicit FDA food-safety review.

EFSA_QPS_list.use: Inclusion list — biennially-updated EFSA Qualified Presumption of Safety microorganism list.

WHO_Fungal_Priority_Pathogens_2022.use: Hard exclusion — 19 species causing serious human invasive disease.

FDA_EFSA_Mycotoxin_DBs.use: Hard exclusion — mycotoxin-producing species (aflatoxin, ochratoxin, fumonisin, trichothecene, ergot, amatoxin).

ClinicalTrials_gov_WHO_ICTRP_ChiCTR.use: Inclusion list — any registered human clinical trial implies regulatory safety review preceded the trial.

translation_models_for_phase_5._principle: Per Open Enzyme/CLAUDE.md §Translation protocol, two-model independent cross-check with inline disagreement annotations. Brian framing 2026-05-06: 'really cheap to use DeepSeek to read those papers' — confirmed: DeepSeek as Model B for Chinese sources is the cheap+native-fluency choice; same heterogeneity-guard pattern the wiki sweep daemon already uses (Pass 4 DeepSeek peer review).

Chinese_sources_CNKI_Wanfang.model_a: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (or Opus 4.7) — primary Western-trained substrate
Chinese_sources_CNKI_Wanfang.model_b: DeepSeek V4-Pro via OpenRouter — Chinese-vendor, native Mandarin fluency, ~$0.14/M tokens (vs. Claude $15-75/M).
Chinese_sources_CNKI_Wanfang.cost_estimate: ~$0.05/paper at typical paper length; thousands of papers = couple hundred USD total — not a real gate.

Japanese_sources_JSTAGE_CiNii.model_b: Gemini 2.5 Pro OR DeepSeek (DeepSeek reasonable Japanese fluency despite Chinese-vendor — the multi-vendor heterogeneity guard is what matters, not language-vendor exact match)

Korean_sources_KISS_RISS.model_b: Gemini 2.5 Pro OR GPT-5 — no Korean-vendor frontier model in standard set; two competent Western-trained models acceptable per CLAUDE.md.

translation_models_for_phase_5.openrouter_proxy: All non-Anthropic models hit via OpenRouter (https://openrouter.ai) — same pattern wiki sweep daemon uses (per Open Enzyme/CLAUDE.md §sweep daemon).

LOTUS.scope: Open natural products with species-of-origin links. ~750K compound-organism pairs across all kingdoms. Filter to fungi (Kingdom=Fungi).
LOTUS.access: Public REST API + bulk CSV/SDF dump on Zenodo (lotusnprod/lotus-data DOI 10.5281/zenodo.5794106).
LOTUS.expected_yield: ~30-80K fungal compound-species records; deduplicate by InChIKey.
LOTUS.language: English metadata, but species-of-origin coverage is global

NPAtlas.scope: Microbial natural products (bacteria + fungi) with strong provenance. ~33K curated entries.
NPAtlas.access: Public REST API + bulk JSON download.
NPAtlas.expected_yield: ~10-15K fungal compounds with literature provenance per record.

KNApSAcK.scope: Japanese-hosted comprehensive species-metabolite database. ~50K species, ~100K metabolites. Strong East-Asian-traditional-medicine coverage including fungi.
KNApSAcK.access: Web interface + downloadable tab-delimited tables. No public REST API — scraping or bulk download required.
KNApSAcK.expected_yield: Higher fungal coverage than Western DBs for East Asian medicinal species (e.g., Ganoderma, Wolfiporia, Antrodia).

NPASS.scope: Natural Product Activity & Species Source DB. ~30K natural products with bioactivity data. China-hosted (NUS / Bidd group).
NPASS.access: Public web interface + bulk CSV download.
NPASS.expected_yield: Mid-thousands fungal compounds with quantitative bioactivity.
NPASS.language: English curation, broader Asian-source coverage than ChEMBL
NPASS.phase_2_verification_needed: URL drift flagged by Phase 1 peer review — bidd.group redirects historically inconsistent. Verify canonical entry point in Phase 2 before committing to this URL in any pull script.

TCMSP.scope: Traditional Chinese Medicine Systems Pharmacology DB. ~30K compounds from Chinese herbs/fungi with predicted targets and ADME.
TCMSP.access: Web interface; bulk export historically inconsistent — may need scraping.
TCMSP.expected_yield: Comprehensive coverage of TCM-relevant fungi (Ganoderma, Wolfiporia, Polyporus, Cordyceps, Tremella, Auricularia).

TCMID.scope: Traditional Chinese Medicine Integrated Database. Compound-disease-target relations.
TCMID.access: Web + downloads. Status: maintenance has lapsed in some periods — verify availability before Phase 2.

TCM_Database_Taiwan.scope: ~32K compounds from Chinese herbs (China Medical University, Taiwan).

HIT.scope: Herb Ingredient Targets database. Curated TCM ingredient → protein target mappings.
HIT.expected_yield: Critical for compounds outside ChEMBL; provides target hypotheses for fungal compounds with TCM literature but no Western pharma curation.
HIT.phase_2_verification_needed: Phase 1 peer review flagged the port-2345 URL as suspect; HIT 2.0 is documented at hit2.badd-cao.net. Verify canonical entry point in Phase 2 before scraping.

BATMAN-TCM.scope: Bioinformatics Analysis Tool for Molecular mechANism of TCM. Predicts targets via similarity to known drugs.

COCONUT.scope: Aggregator of open natural product DBs (~700K compounds). Includes upstream LOTUS, NPAtlas, others.
COCONUT.access: Public REST API + bulk SDF dump.
COCONUT.use_case: Cross-validation / dedup against LOTUS+NPAtlas; not a primary source.

MIBiG.scope: Minimum Information about a Biosynthetic Gene cluster. Curated BGC → product links. ~2.5K entries, fungal subset ~400.
MIBiG.use_case: Genomic-encoded compound coverage including compounds we don't yet have isolation data for.

antiSMASH-DB.scope: Predicted BGCs from sequenced genomes including fungi. ~150K fungal BGCs.
antiSMASH-DB.use_case: Predicted-compound space — for hypothesis generation about uncharacterized chemistry in less-studied fungal genomes.

MycoCosm_JGI.scope: JGI MycoCosm — canonical fungal genomes portal. ~2,000+ assembled fungal genomes with annotated secondary metabolism.
MycoCosm_JGI.access: Public web + bulk download (registration may be required for some downloads).
MycoCosm_JGI.use_case: **Upstream provenance for antiSMASH-DB and MIBiG entries** — without MycoCosm or NCBI RefSeq Fungi as the upstream genome source, predicted-BGC coverage for less-studied candidate species has no provenance back to the producing organism. Added Phase 1 review (peer reviewer flagged as largest source-list gap).

NCBI_RefSeq_Fungi.scope: NCBI RefSeq fungal genomes — backup genomic source for any candidate species not in MycoCosm.
NCBI_RefSeq_Fungi.access: Public via standard NCBI tooling (Entrez, datasets).
NCBI_RefSeq_Fungi.use_case: Phase 2c genome-coverage upstream provenance for candidate species; cross-check against MycoCosm; primary source for any species MycoCosm hasn't picked up.

ChEMBL.scope: European-hosted manually curated bioactivity DB. ~2M compounds, 1.5M assays. Western pharma-skewed.
ChEMBL.access: Public REST + Open Enzyme has MCP plugin (mcp__plugin_chembl_ChEMBL__get_bioactivity, etc.).
ChEMBL.use_case: Empirical IC50 / Ki / Kd against gout-relevant targets where curated.
ChEMBL.limitation: comp-013 surfaced ChEMBL coverage gap for TCM-specific phytochemicals — same gap likely applies to fungal compounds.

PubChem_BioAssay.scope: NIH bioassay corpus, broader than ChEMBL but less curated.
PubChem_BioAssay.use_case: Long-tail bioactivity coverage; cross-check against ChEMBL.

bioactivity_target_databases.HIT: see compound_databases — also a target source
bioactivity_target_databases.BATMAN-TCM: see compound_databases — also a target predictor

SwissTargetPrediction.scope: Similarity-based target prediction for compounds with known structure but no empirical target.
SwissTargetPrediction.use_case: Long-tail orphan-compound target hypotheses. Lower confidence — flag predictions explicitly vs. empirical bioactivity.

PubMed.scope: English-indexed biomedical literature, includes English-translated abstracts of many Asian papers.

CNKI.scope: China National Knowledge Infrastructure. Canonical Chinese-language clinical and pharmacology corpus. The decades of Chinese-medicine clinical experience that ChEMBL/PubMed never see.
CNKI.access: Web interface; institutional subscription typically required for full text. Public abstract access varies.
CNKI.use_case: Phase 3 multilingual literature ingestion — read original-language papers per CLAUDE.md global-multilingual rule.
CNKI.translation_protocol: Per CLAUDE.md §Translation protocol — two-model independent cross-check (one Chinese-vendor model — DeepSeek or Qwen — + one Western model) with inline annotations on disagreement.

Wanfang.scope: Major Chinese academic database, complementary to CNKI.

J-STAGE.scope: Japanese scientific journals — Kampo medicine, mycology, fungal natural products research.
J-STAGE.translation_protocol: Two-model cross-check; ideally include a model with strong Japanese fluency.

CiNii.scope: Japanese academic database, includes fungal/Kampo research.

KISS.scope: Korean Studies Information Service System. Korean-language pharmacology and ethnomycology.
KISS.translation_protocol: Two-model cross-check including a Korean-fluent model.

phase_plan_summary.phase_1_scope: This commit. Inventory sources, candidate species, chokepoint targets. No DB queries executed.
phase_plan_summary.phase_2_breadth_aggregation: Pull LOTUS + NPAtlas + KNApSAcK + NPASS + TCMSP + COCONUT. Dedup by InChIKey. Output: unified fungal compound × species table.
phase_plan_summary.phase_3_target_mapping: Empirical (ChEMBL + HIT + PubChem) → SwissTargetPrediction for orphans. Output: compound × target table with empirical/predicted flag.
phase_plan_summary.phase_4_chokepoint_intersection: Join compound × target × Open Enzyme chokepoints (chokepoint-targets.json). Output: ranked candidate list.
phase_plan_summary.phase_5_multilingual_deep_dive: For top 3-5 species flagged by Phase 4: ingest CNKI + J-STAGE primary literature with two-model translation cross-check. Compare Asian clinical evidence against the breadth-pass mechanism map.
phase_plan_summary.phase_6_per_compound_triage: comp-013-style IC50 occupancy analysis for top candidates. Verdicts: GUT-LUMINAL VIABLE / SYSTEMIC VIABLE / MECHANISM UNCLEAR / NON-VIABLE. Subset gets shio-koji protease stability comp-NNN follow-ups.
